reasoning: The threshold market close price on 2026-01-30 is 107.87. The first resolution date is Feb 8 (Sunday), meaning it will resolve to the Feb 6 Friday close. Notably, CPT is scheduled to release its Q4 2025 earnings after the market closes on Thursday, Feb 5. This makes the Feb 6 close extremely sensitive to the earnings print and guidance. Earnings reactions are effectively coin-flips in the short run unless there are overwhelming headwinds/tailwinds. The stock has shown solid momentum in late January (rising from 105 to 107.87), and historical trends slightly favor upside (the tool's statistical analysis suggested a >50% chance of staying above the threshold). An ex-dividend date was also ruled out as a negative catalyst, as Q1 dividends are typically paid in mid-April with a late March ex-div date (this would not happen before March 3). Balancing the slight upward trend with the heavy unpredictability of the pending Feb 5 earnings release, I've assigned a 53% probability to the Feb 8 date. The March 3 date features a longer forecast horizon, meaning uncertainty increases and the probability regresses closer to 50% (51%).
